title: PWA Manifest, App Icons, and Half-Day Color Indicator Fixes

requirements.summary: Add PWA manifest link, theme-color meta tag, Apple mobile web app meta tags, and Apple touch icon links to index.html
acceptanceCriteria: theme-color meta tag is present with the amber/charcoal app color
apple-mobile-web-app-capable and apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style meta tags are present
Apple touch icon links pointing to generated icon assets are present

file_operations.description: Add `<link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'>` inside the `<head>` section. Add a `<meta name='theme-color' content='#1a1a1a'>` tag matching the app's dark charcoal background. Add Apple mobile web app meta tags: `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-capable' content='yes'>`, `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style' content='black-translucent'>`, and `<meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-title' content='SwipeTrack Pro'>`. Add Apple touch icon links for the generated icon assets at `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-apple-touch-icon.dim_180x180.png` and other iOS sizes (152×152, 120×120) by referencing the generated 192×192 icon file path with appropriate sizes attribute.

requirements.summary: Create a PWA-compliant Web App Manifest with proper metadata and full icon set including maskable variants
acceptanceCriteria: All required manifest fields (name, short_name, start_url, display, theme_color, background_color) are present
Icons array covers all sizes from 48 to 512 including 192 and 512
Icon paths resolve to files that exist under `frontend/public/assets/generated/`

file_operations.description: Create a Web App Manifest with `name: 'SwipeTrack Pro'`, `short_name: 'SwipeTrack'`, `description: 'Office attendance and working-hours tracker'`, `start_url: '/'`, `display: 'standalone'`, `orientation: 'portrait'`, `theme_color: '#1a1a1a'` (dark charcoal), and `background_color: '#1a1a1a'`. Add an `icons` array with entries for all generated icon files: `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-icon-48.dim_48x48.png`, `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-icon-96.dim_96x96.png`, `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-icon-144.dim_144x144.png`, `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-icon-192.dim_192x192.png` (with `purpose: 'maskable any'`), and `frontend/public/assets/generated/swipetrack-icon-maskable.dim_512x512.png` (with `purpose: 'maskable any'`). Also include standard icon entries for 72, 128, 152, 384, and 512 pixel sizes using the appropriate generated assets or the base 512×512 icon. Use `/assets/generated/` as the src path prefix for icon entries to match the public directory structure.

requirements.summary: Register a service worker and create a minimal offline-capable service worker script
acceptanceCriteria: Service worker is registered on app load without errors in the browser console
App shell resources are cached on first load
App loads from cache when network is unavailable
Chrome DevTools Application tab shows the service worker as active

file_operations.description: Add service worker registration logic at the end of the file (after ReactDOM.createRoot call) that checks if `'serviceWorker' in navigator`, then registers `/sw.js` with `navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js')`. Wrap the registration in a conditional check for production mode or always enable it for PWA testing. Log registration success or catch and log any errors. Ensure this does not interfere with the existing Internet Identity authentication flow or ICP agent calls.

Create a minimal service worker script that defines a cache name (e.g., 'swipetrack-v1'), listens to the 'install' event to cache the app shell resources (index.html, main JS/CSS bundles from the build output, and the manifest.json file), and listens to the 'fetch' event to serve cached resources when offline using a cache-first strategy for navigation requests and app shell assets. Include a fallback to network for non-cached resources. Ensure the service worker activates and claims clients immediately on install.

requirements.summary: Fix green/red daily hours color indicator threshold logic for half-day leave entries across DailyEntry and Dashboard pages
acceptanceCriteria: A halfDayFirstHalf entry with 3h 00m calculated hours displays in red with '−1h 30m' shortfall
A halfDayFirstHalf entry with 4h 30m or more displays in green with surplus relative to 4h 30m
A halfDaySecondHalf entry with 4h 00m or more displays in green with surplus relative to 4h 00m
A halfDaySecondHalf entry with 3h 50m displays in red with shortfall relative to 4h 00m
Full-day leave entries show no red indicator regardless of hours value
Regular working day (noLeave) still uses 8h 30m as threshold

file_operations.description: Add a new exported helper function `getTargetHoursForLeaveType(leaveType: LeaveType): number` that returns 270 (4h 30m) for `halfDayFirstHalf`, 240 (4h 00m) for `halfDaySecondHalf`, and 510 (8h 30m) for `noLeave`. Return 0 for `fullDayLeave` to indicate no threshold check should apply. Export this function for use in UI components that display color indicators and surplus/shortfall calculations.

Update the color indicator logic that displays calculated daily hours. Import `getTargetHoursForLeaveType` from `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts`. When rendering the calculated hours display (likely inside the component that shows total hours and the surplus/shortfall), replace the hardcoded 510-minute (8h 30m) threshold with a call to `getTargetHoursForLeaveType(currentRecord.leaveType)`. Compute surplus/shortfall as `calculatedMinutes - targetMinutes`. Apply green styling when `calculatedMinutes >= targetMinutes` and red styling when `calculatedMinutes < targetMinutes`, except for `fullDayLeave` where no red indicator should show regardless of hours (skip color logic if `leaveType === 'fullDayLeave'`).

Update the Today tab and the Week day rows color indicator logic. Import `getTargetHoursForLeaveType` from `frontend/src/utils/hoursCalculation.ts`. In the Today tab section where calculated hours are displayed, replace the hardcoded 8h 30m threshold with `getTargetHoursForLeaveType(todayRecord.leaveType)` and adjust the surplus/shortfall and color logic accordingly (green if >= target, red if < target, no red for fullDayLeave). In the Week day rows breakdown (likely a map over daily records), apply the same logic: call `getTargetHoursForLeaveType(dayRecord.leaveType)` for each day, compute surplus/shortfall relative to that day's target, and apply green/red styling accordingly. Ensure full-day leave entries never show a red indicator.
